check.command: sqlcmd -h -1 -W -Q "SET NOCOUNT ON; SELECT name AS AuditName, predicate AS AuditFilter FROM sys.server_audits WHERE predicate IS NOT NULL;"

candidate_check.description: SQL Server must generate audit records for all direct access to the database(s).

check_content_excerpt:
Determine whether any Server Audits are configured to filter records. From SQL Server Management Studio, execute the following query:
SELECT name AS AuditName, predicate AS AuditFilter
FROM sys.server_audits
WHERE predicate IS NOT NULL

If any audits are returned, review the associated filters. If any direct access to the database(s) is being excluded, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt: Check the system documentation for required SQL Server Audits. Remove any Audit filters that exclude or reduce required auditing. Update filters to ensure direct access is not excluded.
title: SQL Server must generate audit records for all direct access to the database(s).
